name: Shrivatsa Mark
description: The Shrivatsa is a sacred symbol in Hinduism, depicted as a curl of hair on the chest of Vishnu, where his consort Lakshmi resides. It is a mark of divine grace, auspiciousness, and prosperity, representing the eternal bond between Vishnu and Lakshmi. The exact origin of the Shrivatsa is debated, but it is believed to have emerged during the early centuries of the Common Era, becoming a prominent feature in Vishnu iconography. The mark symbolizes Vishnu's role as the preserver and Lakshmi's role as the bestower of wealth and fortune. Its presence on Vishnu's chest signifies his compassionate nature and his willingness to embrace all beings. The Shrivatsa is not inherently a source of power in itself, but rather a symbol of the divine power and blessings that flow from the union of Vishnu and Lakshmi. It is often depicted as a stylized symbol, sometimes resembling a flower or a geometric pattern. The Shrivatsa continues to hold immense significance in Hindu art, sculpture, and religious practices, serving as a constant reminder of the importance of devotion, prosperity, and the eternal presence of the divine couple.
subtitle: Auspicious Symbol of Vishnu and Lakshmi

keyMyths.title: Vishnu's Chest
keyMyths.description: The Shrivatsa is permanently located on Vishnu's chest, symbolizing his eternal connection with Lakshmi. It represents divine grace and abundance.

keyMyths.title: Lakshmi's Abode
keyMyths.description: The Shrivatsa is considered the abode of Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity. Her presence signifies blessings and good fortune.

sources.author: Alain Danielou
sources.description: Explores the symbolism of various Hindu deities and their attributes.
